ops.title: Skill(codex) from schema-bound subagents loses the fork's answer to a bare 'Skill execution completed' stub (StructuredOutput leaks into the fork) — spawn the codex:codex-wrapper agent instead; main-session Skill(codex) is fine

ops.body:
During the anchors-r2 review loop (2026-07-07), 15 of 18 Skill(codex) calls from Workflow wrapper agents returned only "Skill execution completed". Forensics (cc-skills session, 2026-07-08) overturned this memory's original "run it from the main session" reading: every fork spawned and ran 1–6 minutes; the real failure is that workflow `agent(..., {schema})` wrappers leak their `StructuredOutput` return tool into the `context: fork` execution, and a fork that ends its turn on that tool leaves no trailing text for the Skill relay to harvest — the answer is silently discarded (one complete refutation verdict died 2.3s before its wrapper received the stub). Not load, auth, or infra; the main session works only because it has no StructuredOutput tool to leak. Two secondary rots from the same run: forks often self-reviewed as Claude instead of running codex exec (lane-fidelity break), and codex exec refuses non-git working trees without `--skip-git-repo-check`.

**Why:** a harness isolation bug (caller's schema tool visible inside the fork, `toolEndsTurn: true`) plus a relay that treats "no trailing text" as generic success.

**How to apply:** from workflows/subagents, spawn the `codex:codex-wrapper` agent (ships with the codex plugin ≥0.9.0) with the self-contained question as the prompt — never `Skill(codex)` from a subagent. A bare "Skill execution completed" result is a discarded answer, not a success. Main-session `Skill(codex)` stays correct.

Addendum (2026-07-16, cc-context session): registry availability is per-spawn-path, not per-session. `Agent(subagent_type: "codex:codex-wrapper", name: ...)` spawned fine as a named teammate, but the same subagent_type minutes later WITHOUT `name` errored `Agent type 'codex:codex-wrapper' not found` (async-dispatch registry listed only built-ins; no codex skill was loaded either). When the sol lane errors this way mid-run, don't stall or retry — fall back per the Models-table escalation rule (opus xhigh for finder/refuter review lanes; fable for synthesis).
